name: Andhra Pradesh

overview.short: Andhra Pradesh stretches along the southeastern coast of India with a coastline of over 974 km — the second longest in the country. The state is a vibrant tapestry of ancient civilizations, Buddhist heritage, rich agricultural plains fed by the Krishna and Godavari rivers, and a rapidly emerging technology and industrial corridor.
overview.paragraph2: Home to the world-famous Tirupati Balaji temple — the richest and most-visited pilgrimage center on earth — Andhra Pradesh weaves spirituality into its daily fabric. The Eastern Ghats provide a dramatic highland backdrop, while the coast offers untouched beaches and fertile deltas that have sustained civilizations for millennia.
overview.paragraph3: With rapid infrastructure development following bifurcation, Andhra Pradesh is reinventing itself as a forward-looking destination balancing heritage tourism, eco-tourism, and spiritual tourism under the Navaratnalu development vision.
overview.cultureHeritage: The Telugu-speaking people have a literary tradition dating back 11 centuries — Telugu is called the 'Italian of the East' for its melodic quality. The state birthed the Vijayanagara Empire, one of South India's greatest medieval kingdoms. Kuchipudi, a classical dance-drama form originating in the Krishna district, is internationally acclaimed. The Kalamkari craft — intricate hand-painted or block-printed textiles depicting mythological scenes — thrives in Srikalahasti and Machilipatnam.

religiousPlaces.name: Sri Venkateswara Temple (Tirupati)
religiousPlaces.significance: World's richest temple; Lord Vishnu avatar; 10th century CE

religiousPlaces.name: Kanaka Durga Temple
religiousPlaces.significance: Shakti Peetha on Indrakeeladri Hill above Krishna River

religiousPlaces.name: Simhachalam Temple
religiousPlaces.significance: Lord Narasimha; unique idol merged with Shivalingam form

religiousPlaces.name: Srisailam (Mallikarjuna)
religiousPlaces.significance: Jyotirlinga + Shakti Peetha combined; Nallamala forest

religiousPlaces.name: Ahobilam
religiousPlaces.significance: 9 shrines of Lord Narasimha in forest hills

religiousPlaces.name: Mukhalingam
religiousPlaces.significance: 9th century Eastern Ganga dynasty Shiva temples

rivers: Krishna River — 1,400 km; fertile delta; Nagarjuna Sagar dam
Godavari River — Enters AP near Rajahmundry; dramatic gorge; Papikondalu hills
Penna River — Flows through arid Rayalaseema region

waterfalls.name: Talakona Waterfall
waterfalls.note: Tallest in AP; inside Sri Venkateswara National Park; medicinal herbs

waterfalls.name: Ethipothala Falls
waterfalls.note: 3-stream waterfall on Chandravanka River; crocodile sanctuary nearby

mountains: Eastern Ghats (Nallamala Range) — Spans Kurnool–Prakasam; Tiger Reserve; dense forests
Horsley Hills — 1,265m; cool retreat; 150-year-old Eucalyptus tree; Andhra's 'Ooty'
Araku Hills — Eastern Ghats spine; tribal communities; coffee estates

beaches.name: Rushikonda Beach
beaches.highlights: Blue Flag certified; water sports; rocky coves

beaches.name: Ramakrishna Beach (RK Beach)
beaches.highlights: Urban beach; INS Kursura submarine; statue of equality

beaches.name: Bheemunipatnam (Bhimili)
beaches.highlights: Oldest Dutch cemetery in India; lighthouse

beaches.name: Mypadu Beach
beaches.highlights: Olive Ridley turtle nesting site; Pulicat Lake nearby

historicalSites.name: Lepakshi Temple Complex
historicalSites.description: Vijayanagara; hanging pillar; 70+ sculpted pillars; remarkable frescoes

historicalSites.name: Chandragiri Fort
historicalSites.description: Last stronghold of Vijayanagara Empire; Raja Mahal & Rani Mahal

historicalSites.name: Bobbili Fort
historicalSites.description: Vizianagaram district; historical battle of Bobbili site

historicalSites.name: Amaravati Stupa
historicalSites.description: Buddhist art masterpiece; Great Stupa remnants

historicalSites.name: Nagarjunakonda
historicalSites.description: Ikshvaku Buddhist capital; relocated island museum

adventure: Rushikonda Beach Water Sports — Jet skiing, parasailing, banana boat rides, kayaking
Araku Valley Trek — Eastern Ghats tribal trails; coffee estate walks; cave exploration at Borra
Papikondalu Boat Cruise — Godavari river gorge; 2-day/1-night houseboat through dramatic hills
Nagarjuna Sagar Boat Safari — Island monastery exploration
Belum Caves Adventure — 3,229m long; 2nd largest natural cave system in Indian subcontinent
Horseback Riding at Horsley Hills — Hill station trekking and nature walks
Cycling in Amaravati — Buddhist trail by the Krishna River
Mangrove Kayaking at Coringa — Wildlife sanctuary boat tours

famousDishes.name: Pesarattu (moong dosa)
famousDishes.description: Green moong dal crepe, a staple breakfast served with upma and ginger chutney.

famousDishes.name: Gongura Chicken / Mutton
famousDishes.description: Signature Andhra dish cooked with deeply tangy and spicy sour sorrel leaves.

famousDishes.name: Andhra Biryani
famousDishes.description: Unique traditional biryani where marinated meat is cooked with spiced rice.

famousDishes.name: Pulihora (tamarind rice)
famousDishes.description: Tangy tamarind rice, a beloved temple prasadam and festive staple.

famousDishes.name: Bamboo Chicken (Araku specialty)
famousDishes.description: Tribal specialty from Araku; chicken marinated in herbs and cooked inside bamboo over an open flame.

famousDishes.name: Bobbattu (sweet dish)
famousDishes.description: A sweet traditional flatbread bursting with lentil and jaggery filling.

famousDishes.name: Nellore Fish Curry
famousDishes.description: Intensely spiced fish curry from the coastal regions of Nellore.

streetFood.name: Mirchi Bajji
streetFood.description: Banana peppers stuffed and deep-fried to crispy perfection in a gram flour batter.

streetFood.name: Punugulu
streetFood.description: Fried dumplings from fermented rice-lentil batter, served hot with coconut chutney.

streetFood.name: Jonna Rotte
streetFood.description: A healthy, hearty sorghum flatbread typical to the Rayalaseema region.

bestTimeToVisit.conditions: Cool and pleasant. Winter travel is best, especially for the Tirupati festival season.
Very hot, travelers should generally avoid inland regions.

festivals.name: Visakha Utsav
festivals.description: Vizag; cultural festival by the sea

festivals.name: Maha Shivaratri at Srisailam
festivals.description: Massive temple fair; lakhs of pilgrims

travelTips: Tirupati Darshan: Book online at tirumala.org well in advance; TTD manages slotted darshan; hair offering is voluntary but common
Spice Warning: Andhra food is among the spiciest in India; always specify 'less spicy' if needed
Best Araku Access: The Visakhapatnam–Araku Valley train (Kirandul Express) is one of India's most scenic rail journeys — book early
Papikondalu: Houseboats can be booked through AP Tourism; season is October–March
Safety: Generally safe; avoid isolated beach areas at night
Budget: Daily budget traveler ₹800–1,500; mid-range ₹2,500–5,000; luxury ₹8,000+
Local Currency: HDFC, SBI ATMs widely available; carry cash for rural/tribal areas
uniqueFacts: Tirupati Balaji receives approximately ₹650+ crore in donations annually, making it the world's wealthiest temple
Andhra Pradesh has India's second-longest coastline (974 km) after Gujarat
The Borra Caves (Araku) are estimated to be 150 million years old
Lepakshi's 'hanging pillar' — one of 70 carved pillars in the Veerabhadra Temple — appears to be suspended above the floor; a thin piece of paper can be passed under it
The Godavari delta in AP is one of the world's most fertile river deltas and a Ramsar wetland site
Telugu is the 3rd most spoken language in India and is listed as a classical language
Belum Caves (Kurnool) contain the longest cave system accessible to the public in the Indian subcontinent at 3,229 meters
activities: Araku Valley — Trekking and coffee tours
